notes: One gemini-cli session and one claudecode session driven concurrently in the SAME cwd by run-cell-multi.sh, observed by a single --record daemon. The daemon labels each adapter independently (disjoint ProcessMatchers — gemini-cli's CommandPattern bin/gemini vs claudecode's ExactName claude — and disjoint transcript roots — ~/.gemini/tmp vs ~/.claude/projects), so the workspace events.jsonl carries two distinct sessions, one per adapter, each with its own working->ready arc and no cross-contamination. This spec is adapter-agnostic: agentA is whichever session enters working first, agentB the other; both cells (gemini-cli + claudecode) share this expected.jsonl and the same workspace events.jsonl (each cell differs only in which transcript it stores for the replay golden). Every phase anchors on a WORKING transition, never a ready, because both adapters emit a pre-session proc-<PID> row born in ready that never works — anchoring on working skips those placeholders, and new_session on agentB skips agentA's already-matched session. Asserts: two distinct sessions, each an independent working->ready arc, in one workspace.
source: .specs/agent-scenarios.md → Feature: Multiple agents in same workspace

text: First agent's turn drives ready -> working (binds agentA; skips both adapters' born-ready proc-<PID> placeholders by anchoring on working)
First agent's turn completes: working -> ready
The OTHER agent (a DISTINCT session, different adapter, same cwd) drives ready -> working independently (new_session proves it is not agentA; working anchor skips the placeholders)
Second agent's turn completes: working -> ready — two agents, one workspace, labeled independently with no cross-contamination
